evals.prompt: I have a Vite + React + TypeScript app (no Tailwind, uses plain CSS). I want to add Tambo to it so users can chat with an AI that renders components. Walk me through the setup.
evals.expected_output: Detects Vite, detects no Tailwind, uses npm (or detects correct package manager), installs @tambo-ai/react and zod, wires TamboProvider with apiKey AND userKey, creates component registry, adds chat UI via tambo add, sets up @/ path alias for Vite, imports globals.css

evals.prompt: I'm working in a Yarn monorepo with apps/web (Next.js) and apps/api. I want to add Tambo's AI chat to the web app. The monorepo uses Yarn workspaces.
evals.expected_output: Detects Yarn from yarn.lock, detects monorepo from workspaces config, uses yarn workspace commands to install in apps/web, wires TamboProvider with userKey, mentions env var placement gotchas for monorepos

evals.prompt: I have an Excalidraw-like canvas drawing app built with React and Vite. It has global keyboard shortcuts for tools (R for rectangle, T for text, etc). I want to add a Tambo AI chat panel so users can ask the AI to draw things. How do I set this up without breaking the keyboard shortcuts?
evals.expected_output: Detects global keyboard shortcuts concern, includes stopPropagation wrapper for the chat UI, mentions z-index for canvas apps, wires TamboProvider with userKey, suggests defineTool for integrating with the canvas API
